user_message_example: Every mistake I make turns into proof that I am rotten.
user_patterns: I feel guilty all the time
my conscience is eating me alive
I cannot move on from guilt
nietzschean_angle: Nietzsche sees bad conscience as force turned inward. Instead of acting, confronting, or creating, the person becomes their own prison, judge, and executioner.
plain_explanation: This is not healthy responsibility. It is self-attack becoming a way of life. Guilt starts to feel like seriousness, even when it no longer leads to repair or action.
sharp_reply_style: Separate responsibility from self-devouring. Challenge the use of guilt as identity or self-occupation. Do not offer cheap soothing.
primary_references: On the Genealogy of Morality - Bad Conscience and Internalization
Guide - Genealogy, Master and Slave Morality, and Bad Conscience
card_text:
Theme: bad conscience
User example: Every mistake I make turns into proof that I am rotten.
User patterns: I keep beating myself up, I feel guilty all the time, my conscience is eating me alive, I punish myself for mistakes, I cannot move on from guilt
Nietzschean angle: Nietzsche sees bad conscience as force turned inward. Instead of acting, confronting, or creating, the person becomes their own prison, judge, and executioner.
Plain explanation: This is not healthy responsibility. It is self-attack becoming a way of life. Guilt starts to feel like seriousness, even when it no longer leads to repair or action.
Sharp reply style: Separate responsibility from self-devouring. Challenge the use of guilt as identity or self-occupation. Do not offer cheap soothing.
Primary references: On the Genealogy of Morality - Bad Conscience and Internalization, Guide - Bad Conscience Explained, Guide - Genealogy, Master and Slave Morality, and Bad Conscience
Source IDs: genealogy_bad_conscience_excerpt, guide_genealogy_bad_conscience_explained, guide_nietzsche_genealogy_master_slave_bad_conscience
Tags: bad conscience, guilt, self-punishment, internalization, shame
